description.para: Offsets of registers from the start of the device

briefdescription.para: Enable PCAP send data to FPGA every 4 PCAP cycles.
This bit locks security config including: DAP_En, DBGEN,, NIDEN, SPNIEN.
AXI Write Address or Data or response timeout.
AXI Read Address or response timeout.
PCAP to DMA transfer length error.
Detect Positive edge of Init Signal.
Detect Negative edge of Init Signal.

memberdef.name: XDCFG_IXR_ERROR_FLAGS_MASK
memberdef.initializer: (XDCFG_IXR_AXI_WTO_MASK | \ XDCFG_IXR_AXI_WERR_MASK | \ XDCFG_IXR_AXI_RTO_MASK | \ XDCFG_IXR_AXI_RERR_MASK | \ XDCFG_IXR_RX_FIFO_OV_MASK | \ XDCFG_IXR_DMA_CMD_ERR_MASK |\ XDCFG_IXR_DMA_Q_OV_MASK | \ XDCFG_IXR_P2D_LEN_ERR_MASK |\ XDCFG_IXR_PCFG_HMAC_ERR_MASK)

sectiondef.header: DMA Source/Destination Transfer Length Register Bit definitions

briefdescription.para: The handler data type allows the user to define a callback function to respond to interrupt events in the system.

detaileddescription.para: This function is executed in interrupt context, so amount of processing should be minimized.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is unimportant to the driver component, so it is a void pointer.
is the Interrupt status of the XDcfg device.

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each Device Config Interface instance in the system.
Initialize the Device Config Interface driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called before other functions of the driver are called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the config structure.
is the base address for the device. It could be a virtual address if address translation is supported in the system, otherwise it is the physical address.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started.

simplesect.para:
The very first APB access to the Device Configuration Interface block needs to be a write to the UNLOCK register with the value of 0x757BDF0D. This step is to be done once after reset, any other APB access has to come after this. The APB access is considered illegal if the step is not done or if it is done incorrectly. Furthermore, if any of efuse_sec_cfg[5:0] is high, the following additional actions would be carried out. In other words, if all bits are low, the following steps are not done.
AES is disabled
All APB writes disabled
SoC debug fully enabled

briefdescription.para: The functions enables the PCAP interface by setting the PCAP mode bit in the control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: Enable FPGA programming from PCAP interface. Enabling this bit disables all the external interfaces from programming of FPGA except for ICAP. The user needs to ensure that the FPGA is programmed through either PCAP or ICAP.

briefdescription.para: The functions disables the PCAP interface by clearing the PCAP mode bit in the control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit mask data to be written to the Register. The mask definitions are defined in the xdevcfg_hw.h file.

briefdescription.para: The function Clears the specified bit positions of the Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit value which holds the bit positions to be cleared.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Control Register. Use the XDCFG_CTRL_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Lock Register.

detaileddescription.para: These bits can only be set to a 1. They will be cleared after a Power On Reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Lock Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Lock Register. Use the XDCFG_CR_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Configuration Register with the given value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Configuration Register with the given value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Config Register. Use the XDCFG_CFG_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Status Register. Use the XDCFG_STATUS_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the ROM Shadow Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

simplesect.para: This register is can only be written and is used to control the RAM shadow of 32 bit 4K page ROM pages in user mode

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Software ID Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: This register is locked for write once the system enters usermode. Hence API for reading the register only is provided.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the bit mask for the feature in Miscellaneous Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the bit-mask of the feature to be set.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Miscellaneous Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: This register is locked for write once the system enters usermode. Hence API to reading the register only is provided.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if DMA command queue is full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS is the DMA is busy XST_FAILURE if the DMA is idle
The DMA queue has a depth of two.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDcfg *InstancePtr, u32 SourcePtr, u32 DestPtr, u32 SrcWordLength, u32 DestWordLength)
memberdef.name: XDcfg_InitiateDma

briefdescription.para: This function initiates the DMA transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
contains a pointer to the source memory where the data is to be transferred from.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the source transfer.
contains a pointer to the destination memory where the data is to be transferred to.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the Destination transfer.

simplesect.para: It is the responsibility of the caller function to ensure that correct values are passed to this function. The 2 LSBs of the SourcePtr (Source)/ DestPtr (Destination) address when equal to 2�b01 indicates the last DMA command of an overall transfer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDcfg *InstancePtr, u32 SourcePtr, u32 SrcWordLength, u32 DestPtr, u32 DestWordLength)
memberdef.name: XDcfg_PcapReadback

briefdescription.para: This function Implements the DMA Read Command.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to transfer the image data from FPGA to the external memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
contains a pointer to the source memory where the data is to be transferred from.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the source transfer.
contains a pointer to the destination memory where the data is to be transferred to.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the Destination transfer.

simplesect.para:
- XST_INVALID_PARAM if source address/length is invalid.
XST_SUCCESS if DMA transfer initiated properly.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDcfg *InstancePtr, void *SourcePtr, u32 SrcWordLength, void *DestPtr, u32 DestWordLength, u32 TransferType)
memberdef.name: XDcfg_Transfer

briefdescription.para: This function starts the DMA transfer.

detaileddescription.para: This function only starts the operation and returns before the operation may be completed. If the interrupt is enabled, an interrupt will be generated when the operation is completed, otherwise it is necessary to poll the Status register to determine when it is completed. It is the responsibility of the caller to determine when the operation is completed by handling the generated interrupt or polling the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
contains a pointer to the source memory where the data is to be transferred from.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the source transfer.
contains a pointer to the destination memory where the data is to be transferred to.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the Destination transfer.
contains the type of PCAP transfer being requested. The definitions can be found in the xdevcfg.h file.

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM if invalid Source / Destination address is sent or an invalid Source / Destination length is sent

simplesect.para: It is the responsibility of the caller to ensure that the cache is flushed and invalidated both before the DMA operation is started and after the DMA operation completes if the memory pointed to is cached. The caller must also ensure that the pointers contain physical address rather than a virtual address if address translation is being used. The 2 LSBs of the SourcePtr (Source)/ DestPtr (Destination) address when equal to 2�b01 indicates the last DMA command of an overall transfer.

briefdescription.para: Lookup the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the Device Configuration Interface.

detaileddescription.para: This test does a control register write and reads back the same value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XDCFG_INT_* bits defined in xdevcfg_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XDCFG_INT_* bits defined in xdevcfg_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the enabled interrupts read from the Interrupt Mask Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XDCFG_INT_* constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the IMR.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XDCFG_INT_* constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Interrupt Status register.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be cleared. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XDCFG_INT_* bits which are defined in xdevcfg_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: The interrupt handler for the Device Config Interface.

detaileddescription.para: Events are signaled to upper layer for proper handling.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs that needs application's attention.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function perform the reset sequence to the given devcfg interface by configuring the appropriate control bits in the devcfg specific registers the devcfg reset sequence involves the following steps Disable all the interrupts Clear the status Update relevant config registers with reset values Disable the loopback mode and pcap rate enable.

parameterdescription.para: of the interface

simplesect.para: This function will not modify the slcr registers that are relevant for devcfg controller

briefdescription.para: Unlock the Device Config Interface block.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of XDcfg driver.

briefdescription.para: Get the version number of the PS from the Miscellaneous Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of XDcfg driver.

briefdescription.para: Read the multiboot config register value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of XDcfg driver.

briefdescription.para: Selects ICAP interface for reconfiguration after the initial configuration of the PL.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of XDcfg driver.

briefdescription.para: Selects PCAP interface for reconfiguration after the initial configuration of the PL.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of XDcfg driver.
is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XDcfg_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddr, u32 RegOffset)

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XDcfg_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddr, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for XDcfg driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains method for static initialization (compile-time) of the driver.
Contains diagnostic self-test functions for the XDcfg driver.
Contains the implementation of interrupt related functions of the XDcfg driver.
This file contains the hardware interface to the Device Config Interface.
This file contains the implementation of the interface reset functionality.
This file contains a table that specifies the configuration of the Device Configuration Interface device in the system.
Refer to the header file xdevcfg.h for more detailed information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 02/07/11 First release 2.00a nm 05/31/12 Updated the driver for CR 660835 so that input length for source/destination to the XDcfg_InitiateDma, XDcfg_Transfer APIs is words (32 bit) and not bytes. Updated the notes for XDcfg_InitiateDma/XDcfg_Transfer APIs to add information that 2 LSBs of the Source/Destination address when equal to 2�b01 indicate the last DMA command of an overall transfer. Updated the XDcfg_Transfer function to use the Destination Address passed to this API for secure transfers instead of using 0xFFFFFFFF for CR 662197. This issue was resulting in the failure of secure transfers of non-bitstream images. 2.01a nm 08/27/12 Updated the XDcfg_Transfer API to clear the QUARTER_PCAP_RATE_EN bit in the control register for non secure writes for CR 675543. 2.02a nm 01/31/13 Fixed CR# 679335. Added Setting and Clearing the internal PCAP loopback. Removed code for enabling/disabling AES engine as BootROM locks down this setting. Fixed CR# 681976. Skip Checking the PCFG_INIT in case of non-secure DMA loopback. Fixed CR# 699558. XDcfg_Transfer fails to transfer data in loopback mode. 2.03a nm 04/19/13 Fixed CR# 703728. Updated the register definitions as per the latest TRM version UG585 (v1.4) November 16, 2012. 3.0 kpc 21/02/14 Implemented new function XDcfg_ClearControlRegister 3.2 sb 08/25/14 Fixed XDcfg_PcapReadback() function updated driver code with != instead of ==, while checking for Interrupt Status with DMA and PCAP Done Mask ((XDcfg_ReadReg(InstancePtr->Config.BaseAddr, XDCFG_INT_STS_OFFSET) & XDCFG_IXR_D_P_DONE_MASK) != XDCFG_IXR_D_P_DONE_MASK); 3.8 Nava 06/21/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: Each device should have an entry in the table.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 02/07/11 First release

Ver Who Date Changes
2.04a kpc 10/07/13 First release 3.8 Nava 06/21/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.10 Arvd 02/04/26 Fixed codespell errors

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 02/07/11 First release 2.01a nm 08/01/12 Added defines for the PS Version bits, removed the FIFO Flush bits from the Miscellaneous Control Reg 2.03a nm 04/19/13 Fixed CR# 703728. Updated the register definitions as per the latest TRM version UG585 (v1.4) November 16, 2012. 2.04a kpc 10/07/13 Added function prototype. 3.00a kpc 25/02/14 Corrected the XDCFG_BASE_ADDRESS macro value. 3.8 Nava 06/21/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 02/07/11 First release 2.01a nm 07/07/12 Updated the XDcfg_IntrClear function to directly set the mask instead of oring it with the value read from the interrupt status register 3.8 Nava 06/21/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 02/07/11 First release 2.02a nm 02/27/13 Fixed CR# 701348. Peripheral test fails with Running DcfgSelfTestExample() in SECURE bootmode.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 02/07/11 First release 3.5 ms 08/07/17 Fixed compilation warnings. 3.8 Nava 06/21/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.
